name: La Canal de Navarrés Loop
name_original: La Canal de Navarrés — Circular desde Antella
description: This moderate 31.1-mile road cycling loop from Antella explores the natural beauty of La Canal de Navarrés, featuring lakes and waterfalls.
region_intro: Road cycling around Antella offers diverse landscapes, making it a compelling destination for cyclists. The region features varied terrain, including the natural beauty of La Canal de Navarrés with its lakes and waterfalls, and the prominent Xúquer River. Cyclists can navigate routes that range from mostly flat, well-paved surfaces to more challenging paths with significant elevation gain, providing options for different fitness levels. The area is characterized by its natural features and elevated viewpoints like the Balcony of Antella. Best road cycling routes around Antella The most popular road cycling route is La Canal de Navarrés Loop, a 31.1 miles (50.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural beauty of La Canal de Navarrés, an area characterized by its lakes and waterfalls. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Presa de Tous – Barranco de la Mañana loop from Sumacàrcer, an easy 20.6 miles (33.1 km) path. This route offers an easy ride on mostly well-paved surfaces, suitable for various fitness levels. Local road cyclists also love the Finca Tío Ger – Vilanova de Castelló loop from Càrcer, a 34.8 miles (56.0 km) trail leading through the Vilanova de Castelló area, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Antella is defined by its scenic lakes, waterfalls, and the Xúquer River. The network offers options for both easy rides and more challenging routes with notable elevation changes. The routes in Antella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 2,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Antella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Antella?
A: Antella offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 40 different tours to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Antella?
A: The terrain around Antella is quite varied. You'll find routes with mostly well-paved surfaces and gentle elevation gains, suitable for relaxed rides. However, there are also more demanding paths with significant elevation changes, some of which might include unpaved segments, requiring good fitness.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Antella?
A: Yes, Antella has several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39549496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Tous – Barranco de la Mañana loop from Sumacàrcer&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 20.6-mile path on mostly well-paved surfaces. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilanova de Castelló loop from Càrcer&lt;/a&gt;, which is also considered easy.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs near Antella?
A: For cyclists seeking a challenge, Antella offers routes with substantial elevation gain. Some demanding routes can feature up to 3,000 feet of climbing over several hours, testing your fitness. While specific challenging routes are not listed here, you can find 12 difficult tours in the area that will provide a rigorous workout.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling around Antella?
A: The region around Antella is rich in natural beauty. Many routes will take you through La Canal de Navarrés, known for its picturesque lakes and waterfalls. You might also encounter the prominent Xúquer River. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vikings' Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4070836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera de Anna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The area boasts elevated viewpoints offering panoramic vistas. The Balcony of Antella (Balcó d'Antella) on the Creueta Alta mountain provides stunning views of the town, the Vall Farta sub-region, and the Xúquer River. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5825175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salto de Chella Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; along some routes.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes that feature waterfalls or lakes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843732731/la-canal-de-navarres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Canal de Navarrés Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, explores an area characterized by its lakes and waterfalls. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4070836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera de Anna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there loop road cycling routes available in Antella?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Antella are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843732731/la-canal-de-navarres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Canal de Navarrés Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 31.1-mile trail exploring the natural beauty of the area.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Antella?
A: The road cycling routes in Antella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 10 reviews. Over 2,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic lakes, waterfalls, and the Xúquer River.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Antella?
A: While road cycling routes generally require some experience, many of the 'easy' graded routes in Antella are suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances on paved roads. These routes typically have less elevation gain and are on well-maintained surfaces.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Vilanova de Castelló area?
A: Yes, the Vilanova de Castelló area is featured in some road cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39542629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finca Tío Ger – Vilanova de Castelló loop from Càrcer&lt;/a&gt; is a 34.8-mile trail that takes you through this scenic region.

Q: Can I find routes that include the Albufera de Anna?
A: Yes, there is a route that incorporates the beautiful Albufera de Anna. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21987731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolbaite natural pool – Albufera de Anna loop from Sumacàrcer&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 51.7-kilometer ride that features this lake.
tips: A very colorful and picturesque stretch of road to climb to the port of Sumacarcer from the town of the same name. It is a short but demanding section due to its steep slopes in some sectors, a nice and entertaining challenge.

The lake is a protected area of flora and fauna. Even so, it has parking lots and a recreational area.

It is an ideal place to spend a quiet day with the family.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of The Canal de Navarrés — Circular from Antella route?
faqs.answer: This is a moderate road cycling route. It's generally on well-maintained, mostly paved surfaces, with some rural roads featuring very well compacted dirt or asphalt with minimal traffic. A good level of fitness is recommended for cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, The Canal de Navarrés — Circular from Antella is a continuous loop, bringing you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly paved surfaces. You'll encounter well-maintained roads, and some rural sections may have very well compacted dirt or asphalt with minimal traffic, providing a pleasant cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience unforgettable landscapes shaped by water, including pools, waterfalls, and a lake. Notable spots include the Road to Puerto de Sumacàrcer and Albufera de Anna. The region also boasts natural wonders like the Chorradores waterfalls, Playamonte lake, and the river gorges of La Escalera, Gaspar, and Catalán, set amidst pine forests, mastic trees, and kermes oaks.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While enjoyable year-round, spring is particularly recommended for cycling this trail. The weather is generally pleasant, and the natural beauty of the region is at its peak.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Antella?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route from Antella. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Antella, as the starting point, would be the best place to look for local parking options for cyclists. Many rural roads along the route have low traffic, making access generally straightforward.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for cycling The Canal de Navarrés — Circular from Antella route.

faqs.question: Is The Canal de Navarrés — Circular from Antella route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed in many outdoor areas, it's important to consider the safety of your dog on roads, especially if there's any traffic. For road cycling, it's typically not suitable for dogs to run alongside. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including the Camí de la Taronja, El Camí IT5TR6 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, and Abrullador - Fuente Negra - Chella.

faqs.question: Are there any towns or villages along the route where I can find amenities?
faqs.answer: The region is dotted with charming villages such as Anna, Bolbaite, Chella, Millares, and Quesa. While the route itself is rural, these villages offer opportunities for cultural immersion and likely provide amenities like cafes or shops for refreshments.

faqs.question: What is the traffic like on the roads along this route?
faqs.answer: A significant advantage for road cyclists on this route is the generally low or very low traffic on many of the rural roads and asphalt sections, providing a peaceful and safe riding environment.
